_note: AUTHORITATIVE mutable current-state source. The ONLY place current operational state is defined. Mutate via `tools/project-state.py transition`; validate via `tools/project-state.py validate`; generated status blocks are rendered from here via `tools/project-state.py render`. Do not duplicate current-state wording elsewhere outside generated blocks.

delivery_governance.branch_strategy: one complete Phase per branch + one PR (phase/<name>-<purpose>); governance-only uses governance/<purpose>; no direct commits to the protected default branch without explicit PO authorization
delivery_governance.merge_gate: PR merge requires the GitHub Actions 'Project Governance' check (job 'governance') to pass; local validator output alone is insufficient (GH-MANDATORY-CI)

phase3_execution.stage: IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS. INCREMENT 1 (governance + planning) DONE: baseline; read-only architecture + schema audit; connector ADR-0001 (dedicated pmsd owns each PMS Interface socket under a DB advisory single-owner lock, reusing data-plane/internal/pms/protel_fias.go -- NOT a socket inside scd/portald/edged); Phase-3 Plan + Privilege Matrix; Phase-3 governance guards + mutations; D14/T0015; branch + PR #6. INCREMENT 2 (schema + flags) DONE: machine-grounded schema gap audit against a disposable PG16 (all 18 tables exist; corrected design -- episode = stays.lifecycle_version, NO new checkout_episode column; freshness = FOUR independent axes); migration 0010_phase3_stay_resolution up/down (additive, HARDENED: pms_interface_runtime 4-axis table with coherence constraints and NO stored derived_freshness; stays effective_checkout_at + occupancy-evidence columns composite-FK-pinned + all-or-none; site_checkout_grace_config typed scalars in BYTES + canonical device_limit_policy vocab + bounds; auth_resolutions.resolution_request_id; STRICT lifecycle_version episode + one-way stays.status trigger (Post-Stay transitions removed); stay_events full-lineage append-only+terminal trigger + processed_at/review_code; EXECUTE revoked from PUBLIC); authoritative scope-restricted concurrency-guarded runner scripts/edge-migrate.sh (--only required, name-pattern validated, SHA-256 printed, advisory-lock; idempotent via public.schema_migrations); FINAL invariants: stay_events append-first on INSERT + terminal-result rules (APPLIED needs stay_id+processed_at no review_code; MANUAL_REVIEW/FAILED need bounded ^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]{0,63}$ review_code; terminal frozen) reusing the base composite FK; Grace device policy = ONLY REJECT_NEW_DEVICE + typed policy all-or-none + config-jsonb no-duplicate-keys; lifecycle gate 98/98 PASS (runner-twice + raw-reapply-rollback + rollback==pre + reapply==post + INSERT/terminal-lineage/FK/transition/occupancy/runtime/grace/privilege/runner-scope + Part-A secret-generation-pin/event-id-immutability/concurrent-atomic-runner/target-identity/ownership); pms_config.go six Go flags (master/child, fail-closed, nil-repo-when-dark) + table-driven tests PASS; gofmt/vet/build clean; disposable DB destroyed. INCREMENT 3 (pmsd connector runtime): PMSD IMPLEMENTATION UNDER HARDENING - NOT YET ACCEPTED AS COMPLETE. PART A (migration 0010 + runner) COMPLETE incl. final §1-§8 corrections + verified on disposable PG16 (121/121; post-0010 fingerprint b67b581189701193909649044bf5aa6c): A1 pms_interface_runtime.pinned_secret_generation_id + composite FK (same tenant/site/interface), CONNECTED requires pinned revision AND secret generation, identity-only never key material; A2 stay_events.id immutable + catalog-grounded column classification; A3 runner atomic lock-then-ledger (bounded advisory lock, ledger decision only under lock, SKIP_AFTER_LOCK) proven by two concurrent runner processes; runner now enforces MANDATORY positive target identity (--expect-db/--target-kind/--ack-target/--expect-sha256; no db-name blacklist), canonical realpath migration dir, read-only STRUCTURAL ledger verification before the lock (version text NOT NULL PK, applied_at timestamptz NOT NULL, no dup versions, owner allowlist, exact role privileges, 0009 baseline before 0010), explicit SHA-256 checksum, fully-separated standalone --bootstrap-ledger (applies no migration), and deployment-parity svc_scd/edged/portald/acctd/pmsd ZERO-iam_v2-privilege proof; A4 runner target-identity fail-closed (--expect-db, no silent ledger create, explicit --bootstrap-ledger); A5 0010 applies as NOSUPERUSER iam_v2_owner with correct ownership + no PUBLIC grants. PART B partially COMPLETE (build/vet/gofmt/go test -race all GREEN in Docker golang:1.25): §10 cryptographic SHA-256 advisory lock key (namespace stayconnect:pms-interface-owner:v1 || tenant16||site16||interface16, first 8 bytes big-endian signed bigint) with fixed numeric test vectors + malformed-UUID rejection; §14 CLOSED typed error-code vocabulary (DB_UNAVAILABLE/LOCK_*/REVISION_*/SECRET_*/DIAL_*/PROTOCOL_*/RUNTIME_GENERATION_STALE/QUEUE_OVERFLOW/...) with redacting local-diagnostic boundary + canary log tests (password/room/guest/reservation/folio/endpoint never leak into codes); §17 bounded event queue (fixed capacity, back-pressure timeout, QUEUE_OVERFLOW error + overflow->resync latch, no silent drop, context/close drain, zero goroutines). Follow-up hardening (continuation directive §1-§5) DONE + race-green: linearizable Close/Enqueue queue (RWMutex; 100+ concurrent producers + concurrent Close + accounting, no accept-after-close, no leak); TYPED normalized protocol Event (provenance ids + record type + verified external identity + parsed/normalized timestamps + typed Stay/Guest/Folio fields; raw STX/ETX frame bytes never leave the adapter); debug-logging PII gap closed (logs carry only Code + interface UUID + generation + closed-set stage + counters; no raw/redacted err text; canary tests inspect captured logCoded output); migration APPLY role needs only SELECT+INSERT (live-site apply role proven to hold no UPDATE/DELETE/TRUNCATE/REFERENCES/TRIGGER + no public CREATE), rollback/admin separate; bootstrap rejects arbitrary --target-kind. Part-A gate now 121/121. PART B COORDINATED REWRITE COMPLETE + verified (build/vet/gofmt/go test -race all GREEN in Docker golang:1.25): §6 assignment-scoped identity (Deps.LoadAssignment via Ed25519-verified signed appliance assignment; ListActiveInterfaces(ctx,tenant,site); unassigned=zero work; cross-scope rejected; assignment rotation drains+re-scopes); §7 typed Revision.Validate (explicit connector-kind/endpoint/timezone/normalization/read-only/timeouts/heartbeat/freshness/resync; read-only never defaults true) + SecretGeneration + secret load+AES-256-GCM decrypt ONLY after lock (lock loser never decrypts/dials); rotation reconnect; §8 atomic PG AllocateRuntimeGeneration (stored+1, pins revision+secret) + every axis write EXACT compare-and-set (WHERE runtime_generation=expected, not >=); stale owner->RUNTIME_GENERATION_STALE->close transport; §9 independent-axis UpdateTransport/UpdateContinuity/UpdateSync (heartbeat preserves continuity+sync; worker context, no context.Background; persistent DB error closes transport); §10 explicit DB ownership (one shared owned pool, dedicated connection per advisory lock, Repo.Close, lock-watcher stop, per-ownership queue close, supervisor drain); §11 real production-capable injectable FIAS adapter (errLiveDialDeferred REMOVED; reuses internal/pms framing via exported ReadFramedRecord/WriteFramedRecord/ParseFields/record builders -- no second parser; net.Pipe/injected-dialer tests; LS/LD/LR/LA/GI/GC/GO/DR/DS-DE/LE); §12 outbound read-only allowlist at the guardedConn socket-write CHOKEPOINT (PS/PA/unknown write ZERO bytes to the peer; pmsd imports no P#/Posting/Settlement/Payment path); §13 bounded TYPED Event delivery (invalid event rejected before capacity; overflow->GAP_DETECTED+RESYNC_REQUIRED + DR resync request, no silent drop). PART C COMPLETE: disposable PG16 pmsd integration (assignment-scoped discovery, cross-scope rejection, real pg_try_advisory_lock competition, atomic generation N->N+1, stale-owner CAS rejection, independent-axis preservation, zero svc_* iam_v2 grants) via scripts/pmsd-pg-integration.sh; protocol fake-server tests over net.Pipe (startup handshake, typed domain events carrying NO raw STX/ETX bytes + keyed-HMAC evidence, DS/DE resync, write-chokepoint no-PS, queue-overflow->DR) run with -race; mandatory Software CI .github/workflows/phase3-software.yml (gofmt+build+vet+test+-race + migration-0010 gate + PG16 integration; setup failures FAIL not skip). Part-A gate 121/121; PG16 integration 6/6. INCREMENT 3: IMPLEMENTED BUT ACCEPTANCE REOPENED -- AUTHORITATIVE FIAS AND OWNERSHIP HARDENING IN PROGRESS (PO reopened after ffb9f0d: FIAS field map was inverted (RN is ROOM, G# is RESERVATION, GN/GF last/first, GA/GD arrival/departure); connector credential model fabricated an unused Secret for a no-auth Protel transport; event admission was in-memory-before-ownership-CAS; keepalive/idle-LA + DR/DS..DE isolated resync + gap/resync barrier + canonical assignment reuse + lock-loss-before-dial + owner-bound AEAD AAD + atomic config snapshot all require correction). Prior CI (Governance 29684187770 / Software 29684187743 on ffb9f0d) is evidence of the PRIOR test set only, NOT final acceptance. HARDENING PROGRESS (event semantics FINALIZED, race-green in Docker golang:1.25): authoritative FIAS field map (RN=room,G#=reservation,GN/GF last/first,GA/GD arrival/departure) DONE. CONNECTOR-OWNED STAY IDENTITY REMOVED: the unsafe LogicalStayKey is gone from the Event contract and from Event.Validate() as a mandatory identity (a connector cannot own Stay lifecycle -- arrival evidence is correctable within one Stay and the connector has no authoritative lifecycle_version). The remaining resolution hint is renamed StayResolutionCandidate and documented NON-AUTHORITATIVE: not unique, never a database identity, never a lifecycle/Folio/Room decision, never sufficient to apply a Stay mutation (Increment 4 resolves Stay/lifecycle_version transactionally); a valid Event may carry an empty candidate. COMPLETE-RECORD FINGERPRINT: SourceEventFingerprint = dedicated keyed-HMAC (purpose PMS_EVENT_IDENTITY, distinct from evidence/encryption keys; empty key fails closed) over the CANONICAL complete parsed record -- every field code+value in wire order, length-prefixed with boundary separators (no field-boundary ambiguity; duplicate occurrences distinguishable; present-but-empty is not absent), plus record type, interface, normalization version, protocol profile and verified sequence, excluding only STX/ETX framing; exact retransmission is idempotent, any token change is distinct, unknown fields influence the fingerprint but not the typed domain model, and no raw record/PII is logged or persisted outside the keyed boundary. NO SILENT TRUNCATION: RN/G#/GN/GF/FO/GA/GD are carried untruncated and an overlong/control-byte value REJECTS the record (never a shortened, possibly-colliding value reaches the queue/inbox); a rejected record drives continuity to GAP_DETECTED and sync to RESYNC_REQUIRED via the new AxisSink.OnContinuityFault (both axes PERSISTED under the pinned runtime generation -- the previously ignored UpdateContinuity/UpdateSync results are no longer discarded; a DB/generation failure closes the transport instead of continuing over an unrecorded gap) and issues a read-only DR resync request, so the Serve loop never silently continues on a malformed record. Timestamp semantics retained (ArrivalRaw=GA/DepartureRaw=GD are Stay dates, ReceivedAt is the local receipt clock, PMSEventAt only from a verified FIAS event-timestamp field -- never GA; ReceivedAt implies no ordering). Tests added/updated + green: complete-record ordered-pair fingerprint (duplicate/distinct/present-vs-absent/retransmit-idempotent), StayResolutionCandidate non-authoritative (Room Move + arrival-correction keep the same candidate, different reservation differs, candidate is not the fingerprint, empty candidate still validates), per-field overlong rejection with no truncated value reaching the queue, and a Serve-boundary malformed-record test proving OnContinuityFault + DR with zero admitted events. CI-stability: a benign MEASUREMENT race in the pre-existing linearizable-close test (TestQueue_LinearizableClose_NoAcceptAfterClose) was corrected -- the close boundary is now observed BEFORE each Enqueue call (an Enqueue that STARTS after Close() returns must never be accepted), removing a false positive where an Enqueue committed while Close held the write lock and was only observed afterward; race-green x30 in Docker golang:1.25. STRICT INGESTION + ATOMIC GAP STATE + SERIALIZED WRITER (directive items 1-6) DONE + race-green (Docker golang:1.25) + PG16-green: (1) ONE strict FIAS record parser (ParsedRecord) replaces the permissive field parser for ingestion -- exact supported record id, normal trailing pipe accepted, internal-empty (||)/one-char/malformed-code/control-byte tokens rejected with a typed ErrRecordMalformed (never silent skipping), order + duplicates + present-but-empty + unknown-valid codes preserved for fingerprinting; a malformed token drives GAP_DETECTED + RESYNC_REQUIRED and admits zero events. (2) Ambiguous duplicate typed fields fail closed (RN and G# exactly once; GN/GF/FO/GA/GD at most once; no first/last-wins, no map collapse) while unknown duplicates stay fingerprint-only. (3) Derived GuestName removed from the connector Event (original first/last retained; display is an Increment-4/presentation concern, so a valid record never fails because two individually-valid names concatenate past a single-field bound). (4) Gap/resync is now ONE transactional repository operation MarkGapAndRequireResync (both axes or neither, exact runtime_generation CAS, resync_started_at reset for pir_resync_coherent, bounded typed reason persisted in last_sync_failure_code, ErrStaleGeneration on ownership change) -- PG16-proven both-together / forced-failure-rolls-back-both / stale-changes-neither / heartbeat-cannot-clear / normal-writes-preserve; the previously-split UpdateContinuity+UpdateSync gap writes are gone. (5) Comments/docs corrected to the strict complete-record keyed-HMAC (no stale reservation/room/date-only wording). (6) Item F: ONE serialized protocol writer owns EVERY outbound frame (LS/LD/LR startup, LS-ack LA, incoming-LA->LA, idle-LA keepalive, DR, LE); no read-loop/timer/worker touches the socket writer, the request channel is bounded, any activity resets the idle timer, cancellation emits a controlled LE and leaks no goroutine, and a write failure ends the ownership cycle -- race-proven concurrent-requests-serialized + startup/acks + >=3 idle keepalives + link-alive-while-idle + cancellation-no-leak + write-failure-terminates. F INTEGRATION HARDENING (control-frame validation + writer shutdown, this directive items 1-4) DONE + race-green: (1) EVERY inbound frame is STRICT-parsed BEFORE any action (idle reset, heartbeat, ack, resync, dispatch): a malformed/unsupported CONTROL record -- or an unknown record id; the speculative HB record is removed and LE is now a recognized control record -- terminates the ownership cycle with the bounded PROTOCOL_FRAMING_ERROR code and records no heartbeat, no ack and no idle reset, while a malformed DOMAIN record drives the atomic gap/resync, admits zero events, requests DR and keeps the barrier active. (2) Cancellation shutdown is prompt and bounded: the sole writer emits a controlled LE within the write deadline then CLOSES the transport to release a reader parked behind a multi-minute heartbeat timeout, so Serve returns within a short bound independent of HeartbeatTimeout, with no goroutine leak (proven with HeartbeatTimeout set to minutes). (3) The writer exposes context-aware Submit(ctx)/SubmitSync(ctx); all critical frames (startup LS/LD/LR, LS/LA acks, DR) are written SYNCHRONOUSLY so a write failure is observed immediately, the request channel stays bounded, and no caller blocks indefinitely behind a stalled socket. (4) Post-start writer-failure coverage proves each of the LS-ack LA, LA-ack LA, idle-keepalive LA and DR writes (and a failed shutdown LE) terminates Serve, closes the transport, releases the reader and leaks no goroutine. G DATA MODEL (durable resync inbox, reuse stay_events; Migration 0010 amended, undeployed) DONE + lifecycle-gate 121/121 + PG16-green: stay_events reused as the append-first durable inbox with additive immutable columns admission_kind (LIVE|RESYNC), admission_runtime_generation, resync_generation (0 for LIVE, >0 for RESYNC), fingerprint_key_version; a se_admission_coherent CHECK; and admission-aware idempotency -- the baseline UNIQUE(scope,external_event_identity) is replaced by TWO partial unique indexes (LIVE dedup per interface; RESYNC dedup per resync generation), so a fresh full roster can restage a record whose content matches a LIVE row. pms_interface_runtime gains resync_generation_seq (monotonic typed-resync allocator, bumped per DS) and published_resync_generation (the ATOMIC single-row PUBLICATION boundary advanced only at a valid DE) with a pir_resync_generation_coherent CHECK (0 <= published <= seq). RESYNC Event rows are IMMUTABLE: activation is NOT a mass row update -- a staged generation becomes consumable only when the one runtime-row boundary reaches its resync_generation, and a partial/failed generation (no DE) leaves the boundary unchanged so its rows stay isolated for deterministic cleanup. The append-only trigger extends its immutable-column set to the admission columns. up/down/reapply + rollback-catalog-equality + owner-parity + zero-service-grant all pass on disposable PG16; a new resync table was NOT needed (stay_events proven sufficient). G PERSISTENCE LAYER DONE + PG16-green + race-green: Repo.AllocateResyncGeneration (monotonic seq bump under exact runtime-generation CAS), AdmitLiveEvent + StageResyncEvent (append-first INSERT inside ONE tx that first PROVES ownership of the exact runtime generation via SELECT..FOR UPDATE on the runtime row, exposing only the durable row id), and PublishResyncGeneration (advance published_resync_generation in ONE atomic row update + mark IN_SYNC/CONTINUOUS, over-publish rejected, CAS-guarded). New PG16 integration tests prove monotonic allocation, staged-invisible-before-publication, atomic-publication-makes-consumable, over-publish-rejected, stale-owner-does-nothing, LIVE-vs-RESYNC partial-unique idempotency, and admission-column immutability. G STATE MACHINE + H APPLICATION BARRIER + DURABLE LIVE ADMISSION DONE + race-green + PG16-green: the worker sink now enforces the barrier and drives the full DS..DE resync lifecycle. RequireInitialResync raises the barrier at connect (sync->RESYNC_REQUIRED) and the adapter sends the INITIAL DR through the single serialized writer; DS (OnResyncStart) allocates a NEW typed resync generation under the exact runtime-generation CAS and opens the staging window; every GI/GC/GO inside the window is StageResyncEvent (immutable RESYNC row, invisible to the Stay engine); DE (OnResyncComplete) atomically PublishResyncGeneration (ONE runtime-row boundary update + IN_SYNC/CONTINUOUS) and only THEN lowers the barrier -- a DE without a DS, or a fault/disconnect before DE, publishes nothing and keeps the barrier up. While the barrier is up (not synced, no window) LIVE records are HELD (zero admission; heartbeat/LA cannot lower it). Once synced, OnDomainEvent does durable AdmitLiveEvent (append-first INSERT under a synchronous ownership proof via SELECT..FOR UPDATE on the runtime row + exact runtime-generation CAS) then records feed continuity; a stale owner admits/stages nothing. In-memory-queue overflow no longer forces a gap -- the durable stay_events row is the AUTHORITATIVE store and the bounded queue is only a benign best-effort wakeup. Sink tests (fakeRepo): held-before-publish, staged-in-window, heartbeat-cannot-lower-barrier, valid-DE-publishes-then-live, no-publish-without-DE, DE-without-DS-is-noop, stale-owner-admits-nothing; adapter test: initial-DR-after-connect; plus a PG16 end-to-end sink lifecycle (held->staged->published->consumable->live) through the real append-only triggers. CI-stability: fixed a pre-existing concurrency bug in internal/localkeys.CreateKeyIfAbsent -- a losing concurrent bootstrapper read the winner mid-write O_EXCL empty file and wrongly failed too-short instead of falling through to the race-safe create; 300x race-stress (Docker golang:1.25, GOMAXPROCS=2) green. CREDENTIAL_MODE (truthful NONE) + MIGRATION-0010 CREDENTIAL-AWARE PIN COHERENCE DONE + race-green + lifecycle-gate 121/121 + PG16-green: Revision carries an EXPLICIT credential_mode (NONE|AUTH_KEY); rev.Validate requires it explicit and fails closed -- a NONE connector must carry NO secret generation (no fabricated secret for the no-auth Protel FIAS transport) and an AUTH_KEY connector must carry one. The worker loads/validates/decrypts a Secret Generation ONLY for AUTH_KEY; a NONE connector dials the no-auth transport with no secret material (spy-proven: dial without decrypt). AllocateRuntimeGeneration pins credential_mode (fail-closed default AUTH_KEY when unspecified); LoadInterface parses config->auth->>credential_mode (fail-closed default AUTH_KEY). Migration 0010 adds pms_interface_runtime.credential_mode and makes pir_connected_pins CREDENTIAL-AWARE: CONNECTED requires a pinned Secret Generation ONLY for AUTH_KEY (a NONE row is legitimately CONNECTED with none). PG16-proven: NONE CONNECTED-without-secret allowed; AUTH_KEY CONNECTED-without-secret rejected by the CHECK. REMAINING (next coherent unit): canonical assignment/trust-registry reuse + fail-closed drain, atomic config snapshots, Revision/credential rotation, lock-backend-loss-before-dial, owner-bound AES-GCM AAD for credentialed connectors, startup validation, panic cleanup/replacement, and the expanded PG16/protocol/race gate. Increments 4-9 REMAIN (Increment 4 Stay/Event/Sharer/Folio engine; 5 STRICT multi-PMS resolver D1-D11; 6 PMS Auth Context + Phase-2 Commerce; 7 Room Move/Checkout Grace/Emergency Grace/Reinstatement F1-F7; 8 Guest Portal + Hotel Admin; 9 controlled live read-only PMS verification + live-dark deployment + reboot + final evidence). Phase 3 NOT accepted/closed; all flags OFF; no appliance/Production/live-PMS contact; awaiting Increments 4-9 + one final Product-Owner acceptance decision. PRIOR increment-3 skeleton (advanced/testable, superseded by Part B): data-plane/cmd/pmsd + internal/pmsd (Deps-injected Run, supervisor one-worker-per-interface, worker ordered ownership lock->re-read->startup-persist->dial->serve, single-owner advisory Locker + pgLocker, typed Repo + pgRepo with optimistic runtime_generation upsert, three-axis freshness persistence, FIAS outbound allowlist rejecting PS/PA reusing internal/pms -- no second stack, sanitized bounded error codes) + deploy/systemd/stayconnect-pmsd.service (least-privilege: dedicated user, NoNewPrivileges, empty caps, PrivateTmp/ProtectHome/ProtectSystem=strict, Restart=on-failure so DARK never storms); flags-OFF => zero DB/repo/locker/socket/worker (spy-proven); go build/vet/gofmt clean + go test -race PASS on Linux (flags-off zero-side-effect, fail-closed, one/two-worker, competing-owner-no-socket, disabled-no-dial, revision-reread, lock-loss-closes-socket, stale-generation-rejected, startup-no-fake-HEALTHY, outbound-no-PS, sanitize-no-PII, backoff-bounded, SIGTERM-no-goroutine-leak, one-failure-isolation, lock-key-deterministic). No live PMS connection (dark; live dial deferred to increment 9). NOT YET DONE (increments 4-8): Stay/Event/Folio engine; STRICT resolver; PMS Auth Context; Commerce integration; Room Move; Checkout Grace + emergency fallback; Reinstatement; Guest Portal + Hotel Admin UI; D1-D11/F1-F7 tests; and (increment 9) live READ-ONLY PMS verification; live-dark deployment + reboot; final acceptance report. All Phase-3 flags OFF; no PMS connection; zero Phase-3 SQL in production; no runtime iam_v2 grants; no PMS financial posting; no live guest use; no Production DB accessed (all schema work on a disposable container).

database_schema_state.public_structural_fingerprint_phase1a_md: MD5 of sorted public-schema {columns + constraints + indexes} excluding the Phase-1A MG-0 anchor index guest_networks_tsi_anchor (iam_v2_scratch/prod_live_dark.sh). Phase-1A baseline; PRE-Phase-1B / pre-0007-0008.
database_schema_state.public_columns_sha256_algorithm: Phase-1B/Gate-P value. SHA-256 over information_schema.columns table.column:data_type, computed by the Gate-P tooling (iam_v2_scratch/prod_live_dark.sh). Distinct algorithm from the Phase-1A MD5 above; NOT comparable to it.
database_schema_state.public_columns_phase2_livedark_algorithm: Phase-2 live-dark value. encode(digest(string_agg(table_name||'.'||column_name||':'||data_type, chr(10) ORDER BY 1),'sha256'),'hex') over information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='public', computed read-only on 172.21.60.23 during the Phase-2 live-dark deploy.
database_schema_state.public_columns_fingerprint_reconciliatio: public_columns_sha256_current (7241b1cd, Phase-1B/Gate-P) and public_columns_phase2_livedark_sha256 (833c3d67, Phase-2 live-dark) use DIFFERENT serializations (per-object Gate-P method vs aggregate string_agg+newline+digest). They were NOT re-verified against each other on the appliance (this reconciliation does not touch the appliance), so they are recorded as SEPARATE, NOT-directly-comparable fields. The Phase-2 darkness invariant is that the 833c3d67 value is IDENTICAL pre- and post-migration-0009 (public schema unchanged by 0009) -- proven in docs/evidence/StayConnect-IAM-Phase2-Live-Dark-Evidence.md -- not equality between the two named fields.
database_schema_state.public_structure_change_note: The Phase-1A structural baseline was intentionally superseded by the AUTHORIZED additive migrations 0007/0008 (auth_throttle_buckets, otp_hmac_key_generations, auth_otps.otp_key_generation). The pre->post SHA-256 delta (8659c08d -> 7241b1cd) is exactly those additive objects; this is authorized schema evolution, not drift.
database_schema_state.migration_0009_note: Phase-2 additive iam_v2 migration 0009_phase2_commerce applied live on 172.21.60.23 (2 trigger functions + 2 triggers + 6 lookup indexes, owned by iam_v2_owner). The public-schema columns SHA-256 is unchanged pre->post (public schema untouched); iam_v2 remains 0 rows.
database_schema_state.standby: none (an isolated second-site TEST db stayconnect_site_b exists for isolation tests; NOT a replication standby)

service_routing_state: Legacy public-schema IAM is the SOLE production authority. No service is routed to iam_v2. All four site-DB daemons (scd/edged/acctd/netd) now connect under dedicated LEAST-PRIVILEGE Gate-P roles (svc_scd/svc_edged/svc_acctd/svc_netd; NOSUPERUSER, SCRAM) instead of superuser stayconnect — reboot-validated 2026-07-18. All new auth features (durable throttle, keyed-HMAC OTP, IAM-v2) remain OFF (dark).
pms_financial_state: FIAS lookup-only in production; posting engine is a future component (Phase 4). folio_identity_strategy DEFAULT 'UNSET' fail-closed. No programmatic reversal (v1). Hotel ID 2 financially UNAPPROVED.
network_ha_state: Two physical NICs (WAN=management, LAN=guest). Local-first/offline supported. HA failover NOT designed/implemented/accepted; HA-sync transport is an open decision.
blockers: No governance blocker: Phase 3 (PMS Stay Domain, STRICT resolution, Room Move, Checkout Grace, Reinstatement) is Product-Owner AUTHORIZED (decision D14, start transition T0015) and IMPLEMENTATION IN PROGRESS as one end-to-end Phase, DARK. Phase 1A/1B/2 remain ACCEPTED_AND_CLOSED. Two external live-action dependencies apply only to LATER Phase-3 workstreams (not this planning increment): appliance SSH access for the eventual live-dark deployment (Section 42), and a controlled PMS test reservation created by authorized hotel staff for live READ-ONLY verification (Section 39). Phase 4 remains NOT_STARTED and unauthorized.

acceptance_candidate_head_note: acceptance_candidate_head == inventory_head: the committed HEAD immediately before the final delivery-only commit, and the generation/provenance HEAD recorded in docs/manifests/Phase3-change-manifest.md. The manifest's complete path/status set describes base..delivery_head (the current Phase-3 PR HEAD); the delivery-only commit that adds the regenerated manifest + rebuilt export packs + this pointer sits one commit above inventory_head and therefore cannot appear inside its own generated manifest. delivery_head_note: delivery_head (the current Phase-3 PR HEAD) = inventory_head + one delivery-only commit; its SHA is recorded in the PR #6 body and the Phase-3 report, not self-referenced inside a committed artifact.
